message.content:
@startuml
' Enums are represented as classes with <<enumeration>> stereotype for standard UML compliance.
class Wood {
    CEDAR
    MAHOGANY
    ALDER
} <<enumeration>

class Strings {
    SIX
    TWELVE
} <<enumeration>

class Type {
    ACOUSTIC
    ELECTRIC
} <<enumeration>

class Builder {
    FENDER
    GIBSON
    MARTIN
    COLLINGS
} <<enumeration>

class GuitarSpec {
  - builder : Builder
  - model : String
  - type : Type
  - numStrings : int
  - backWood : Wood
  - topWood : Wood
  
  + getBuilder() : Builder
  + getModel() : String
  + getType() : Type
  + getNumStrings() : int
  + getBackWood() : Wood
  + getTopWood() : Wood
}

class Guitar {
  - serialNumber : String
  - price : double
  - builder : Builder
  - model : String
  - type : Type
  - backWood : Wood
  - topWood : Wood
  
  + Guitar(serialNumber:String,price:double,builder:Builder,model:String,type:Type,backWood:Wood,topWood:Wood) 
  + getSerialNumber():String
  + getPrice():double
  + setPrice(double):void
}

Guitar --|> "Has a" GuitarSpec 

note right of this link
0..*
end note


class Inventory{
- guitars:List<Guitar>  
+getGuitar(String serialNumber):Guitar   
+search(Guitar searchGuitar):Guitar    
Inventory ()     
addGuitar(string serailnumber,double price,Builer buider,string modle ,type typpe wood bakwood woood toopwod )  

 }



guitars --> inventory contains 


@enduml
